_usage: FAQPage template, filled from the real /security page FAQ (the five Q&As in its 'Frequently asked questions' section). Emit on any page that carries an FAQ section — Home, Platform, How-it-works, Security, Open-source, Pricing, Developers, About, all four Solutions, all six Why-Oraclous pages, and all eleven platform capability pages (see faq-blocks.md for every page's exact Q&As). Each acceptedAnswer.text must be the page's actual FAQ answer, in plain text (strip Markdown links; keep exact terminology — ReBAC never RBAC, Agent never bot). Google restricts FAQ rich results to govt/health sites, but AI engines still parse FAQPage for question-answer extraction, so implement it everywhere. Do not duplicate the same Q&A across pages with different answers — faq-blocks.md flags the near-duplicate clusters to keep canonical.

mainEntity.name: How do you control what an AI agent can access?

acceptedAnswer.text: Through ReBAC — relationship-based access control enforced at the Harness level — plus a versioned policy set per run that caps tokens, wall-time, and tool calls and declares a capability allow/denylist. Coded enforcement overrides any prose instruction, so an agent cannot expand its own permissions.

mainEntity.name: Can hosted vendors (including Oraclous) see my data?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. In cloud-hosted mode, customer state is encrypted under customer-controlled keys and KMS separation means Oraclous staff cannot decrypt it by virtue of operating the platform (ADR-008). Support and debugging happen with your participation, not in your absence.

mainEntity.name: Can AI agents run air-gapped or on-premise?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Oraclous is open source and platform-as-code, so it self-hosts on your own infrastructure, including air-gapped environments, with the same data-sovereignty guarantees as the cloud mode. The difference between modes is operational, not architectural.

mainEntity.name: Is there a HIPAA or SOC 2 AI agent platform here today?

acceptedAnswer.text: Oraclous provides architectural data sovereignty — structural org isolation and customer-held keys — today. ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II are on the roadmap and not yet certified, and we say so rather than implying certifications we do not hold. Self-hosting lets regulated teams meet their own control requirements directly.

mainEntity.name: What is the most secure way to run AI agents for regulated industries?

acceptedAnswer.text: Run them where isolation is structural and you hold the keys. Oraclous makes cross-organisation data flow impossible at the data layer, keeps credentials encrypted in a broker that never releases them in plaintext, and lets you self-host — including air-gapped — with the same guarantees as the cloud.
